title: Compress this market sizing analysis to the number that matters and why
guidance: Reward the version that gives a partner the number, the logic, and the critical assumption — not the one that restates the methodology.

parts.label: Supporting logic
parts.note: 3 bullets: bottoms-up logic, key assumption, growth driver

runner_input.instruction: Compress the market sizing analysis below to its key number and the logic behind it for a partner briefing slide.
runner_input.constraints: Max 200 words. State the addressable market, the critical assumption, and the primary growth driver. Skip methodology detail.

source_blocks.text: UK B2B compliance and regulatory-reporting software market. Bottom-up sizing from a survey of 18,000 mid-market firms (50–500 employees). Average contract value benchmarked at £12k/year for a full compliance-suite subscription.

items: Current penetration: ~4% (dedicated software); remainder still using spreadsheets or outsourced advisors
Adjacent market (HR compliance overlay): ~£380m; 60% customer overlap with compliance buyers
Primary growth driver: ESG reporting mandate (UK CSRD equivalent) expected 2026 — will force mid-market firms to adopt structured reporting for the first time
Serviceable addressable (realistic ICP): £78m (firms with 100–500 employees, recurring IT budget >£50k)

rubric_notes: The number that matters is the SAM (£78m), not the TAM (£216m) — the ICP filter is the critical assumption.
The ESG mandate growth driver is the most actionable insight for investment timing.
Penalise responses that lead with the adjacent market (£380m) without explaining why it is not the core opportunity.
